title: solidago virgaurea subsp. lapponica
intro: Solidago virgaurea subsp. lapponica, also known as Arctic goldenrod, is a subspecies of the Solidago virgaurea plant, a perennial herb native to the Arctic regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight off infections. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain and arthritis symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Solidago virgaurea subsp. lapponica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and aid in the recovery from illness.
